3.why: There is no value to build a sequence from.
3.invariant: An empty array has no run.

3.happening: The array has values, so we begin searching.
3.why: Every value could start a consecutive run.

4.why: An empty array has no consecutive sequence.

5.happening: Set the best run length to 0.
5.why: We will grow it as runs are found.

6.happening: Take value {num} as a possible run start.
6.why: The naive method tries starting from every value.
6.invariant: Each value gets its own trial run.

7.why: current will climb through consecutive values.
7.invariant: current marks the head of this run.

8.happening: This run has length 1 so far.
8.why: It contains only the starting value.
8.invariant: length counts values in the current run.

9.happening: Look for {current}+1 somewhere in the array.
9.why: If the next value exists, the run can extend.
9.invariant: A run extends only while the next value is present.

10.happening: Found it â€” step up to {current}+1.
10.why: The consecutive value exists, so the run grows.
10.invariant: current always holds the run's head.

11.happening: The run is now one longer.
11.why: We counted the newly reached value.
11.invariant: length equals values walked from the start.

12.happening: Compare this run against the best so far.
12.why: We keep the longest run seen.
12.invariant: longest is the max over all trial runs.

13.why: Every value has been tried as a start.
13.invariant: The answer is the longest run found.

loop.happening: Pick a start value or probe the next one.

update.happening: Grow the run and record the best.
update.why: Track how far this run reaches.

move.happening: Step the head up to the next value.
